fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Dakota Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity north dakota, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to north dakota, speech-language pathologist compact north dakota, north dakota speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners on Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: North Dakota Board of Examiners on Audiology and SLP

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in North Dakota.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Access the North Dakota Licensing Portal at https://www.nd.gov/eforms/DoD/DoDSearch.aspx?ID=394 and create a new user account if you do not already have one. Log in and select 'New Application' for Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the Speech-Language Pathology application form directly within the North Dakota Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official graduate transcripts, showing the conferred degree, to be sent directly from your university to the NDBESLPA office.

steps.title: Provide PRAXIS Exam Scores
steps.description: Have your official Speech-Language Pathology Praxis exam scores (Code 0017 or 5183) sent directly to the NDBESLPA from ETS.

steps.title: Submit Clinical Fellowship (CF) Documentation
steps.description: Provide documentation of supervised postgraduate professional experience, such as the ASHA Clinical Fellowship Report and Rating Form, directly to the NDBESLPA.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required initial application fee through the North Dakota Licensing Portal to finalize your application.

telepractice_text: North Dakota permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. North Dakota is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: North Dakota issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: North Dakota does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a North Dakota SLP license cost?
Is North Dakota part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does North Dakota allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid ND license.

faqs.question: Does North Dakota have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. North Dakota participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in North Dakota without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: North Dakota does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in North Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in North Dakota, you must submit your application online through the North Dakota Licensing Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the North Dakota Licensing Portal and click 'Create Account' to register a new user account. Once registered, you can log in to access the application forms.
